name: How we solved Context Management in Agents — video interpretation (Sally-Ann DeLucia)
context_primitives: Speaker: Sally-Ann DeLucia, Head of Product at Arize, core contributor to **Alex** (an AI harness / agent for building AI apps on top of Arize observability—advanced planning, 40+ skills, workflows for prompt optimization, data gen, augmentation, annotations). Thesis: **context engineering** (strategically choosing what the model sees) has overtaken prompt-only thinking; best strategy is letting the agent **remember what it needs and forget what it does not**. Problem: Alex consumes **traces/spans**; one trace is already huge; cross-trace patterns multiply—context is a **product and UX** problem, not only engineering. **Vicious loop:** Alex runs on trace/span data → spans grow → context limit → failure is recorded in spans → retries add more data → repeat. **Failed approaches:** (1) naive truncation (first ~100 chars only)—broke reasoning and follow-ups felt like new chats; (2) LLM summarization of full context—too inconsistent, no control over salience. **Production approach ('smart truncation + memory'):** keep **head ~100 chars + tail ~100 chars**, remove middle into a **memory store** the agent can query; dedupe; keep **latest** long tool results; **do not reset** system prompt; middle truncated. Framing: **context** = what the model sees now; **memory** = what survives for retrieval. **Long sessions:** users stay in one chat across the app; failures surfaced late—mitigation **long-session evals** (e.g. load 10 turns, test the 11th) so context bugs are testable before user reports. **Sub-agents:** heavy search over hundreds of spans moved out of main thread—main chat stays light; sub-agent holds heavy intermediate context; results passed back; optional memory retrieval. **Open issues:** very large prompts still hit provider limits; pattern continues to be **more sub-agents**; **long-term memory** still in progress (new chat lacks prior issues); context selection still **heuristic** (no principled context budget / context-quality metrics—evals used as proxy); speaker notes **Claude Code** public discussion describes a **similar** head/tail truncation style. **Q&A:** retrieval of dropped segments via **DB IDs** + tool that previews by position in conversation; cache sophistication deferred in favor of long-term memory work.
deployment_pattern: Implement head/tail window + externalized middle with tool-based recall; add long-horizon eval harness for turn N+1 after N-turn prefix; split data-heavy paths into sub-agents with narrow transcripts; store retrievable blobs with stable IDs for audit and re-fetch.
benchmarks_eval_alignment: Speaker describes **long-session evals**: synthetic multi-turn loads (example: 10 turns then evaluate turn 11) to detect late degradation of context policy—orthogonal to academic NIAH but aligned with production agent QA.
ecosystem_integrations: Arize observability (traces/spans) as the data plane Alex operates on; sub-agent orchestration; external DB for truncated-middle storage and ID-based recall; comparison point to Claude Code context handling discussed in the community.
differentiators: First-hand account of building an **observability-native** agent where the **instrumentation is also the workload**—unusual severity of context growth vs. generic chatbots.
significance_for_builders: Prioritize **evals over vibes** for long chats; treat summarization as non-default until controllable; separate **working context** from **durable retrievable memory**; use **delegation** to cap main-thread tokens when tool chains explode.
key_timeline_or_milestones: Talk references mid-2024 rise of 'context engineering' term and Karpathy '+1 context engineering over prompt engineering' meme; Alex smart-truncation approach stable for 'a few months' at time of talk; team revisiting strategy as limits reappear.

primary_sources.title: YouTube — How we solved Context Management in Agents (video)
Arize — Managing LLM context windows for AI agents
